data.title: Carpenter Technology Q2 earnings beat: EPS $2.33 (est $2.21), revenue up $51.10M, released Jan 29, 2026 - Benzinga.com.
data.description: Carpenter Technology (NYSE: CRS) released its Q2 earnings report on Thursday, January 29, 2026, at 08:00 AM. They surpassed estimated earnings by 5.0%, with an EPS of $2.33 compared to the estimated $2.21. Revenue saw an increase of $51.10 million from the previous year. For more detailed information, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Visa's Q1 earnings expected to be $3.14 per share, revenue $10.69 billion. Can earn $500/month from its dividends.
data.description: Visa Inc. (NYSE: V) is set to announce its Q1 earnings on Jan. 29. Analysts predict earnings of $3.14 per share, up from $2.75 last year. Revenue is anticipated to reach $10.69 billion, compared to $9.51 billion in the previous year. Visa's dividend yield stands at 0.82%, with a quarterly payout of 67 cents per share ($2.68 annually). Investors eyeing $500 monthly or $6,000 yearly from dividends would require a significant investment. For more details, the full story is on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Fortrea (Nasdaq: FTRE) to release Q4 and full year 2025 financial results on Feb 26, 2026, with a conference call at 8:00 am ET.
data.description: In Durham, North Carolina, on January 29, 2026, Fortrea (Nasdaq: FTRE), a prominent worldwide contract research organization, disclosed that the company is set to reveal its financial results for the fourth quarter and entire year of 2025 prior to market commencement on Thursday, February 26, 2026. Fortrea has scheduled a conference call at 8:00 am Eastern Time on the same day to discuss the financial outcomes and engage in a question-and-answer segment.

data.title: New home listings in the US increased by 1% in Jan, first rise in over 2 months, linked to improving demand and lower mortgage rates.
data.description: In Seattle, new data has indicated a 1% increase in the number of U.S. homes for sale compared to the same period last year, over the four weeks ending January 25. This marks the first uptick in over two months as reported by Redfin, a real estate brokerage partnered with Rocket. The rise in new listings is attributed to a growing demand for homebuying. U.S. pending home sales experienced a moderate decline of 1.6% year over year, the smallest drop in almost two months, while mortgage-purchase applications also showed positive trends.

data.title: KnowBe4 marks 10 years of AI innovation with 7 active agents, including AIDA, as a leader in cybersecurity industry.
data.description: TAMPA BAY, Fla.-- KnowBe4, a globally recognized platform focusing on human and AI risk management, is commemorating ten years of leading the way in Artificial Intelligence (AI) for cybersecurity. The milestone being celebrated is the ten-year anniversary of the beta release of AIDA (Artificial Intelligence Defense Agents). This marks KnowBe4 as a pioneer in training both humans and AI agents, solidifying its status as the sole agentic provider in the field. Additionally, Harlan Parrott has recently been appointed to a significant role within the organization.

data.title: BitGo is now first public, federally chartered digital asset company (NYSE: BTGO) after IPO and OCC approval.
data.description: BitGo Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: BTGO) based in New York, has made history by becoming the first federally chartered digital asset infrastructure company to go public. This milestone was achieved after its successful initial public offering and subsequent listing on the New York Stock Exchange on January 22, 2026. Furthermore, BitGo received final approval from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) to function as a national trust bank. These dual achievements have positioned BitGo at the forefront of the digital asset industry.

data.title: ALT5 Sigma Corp will pursue a $100 million stock buyback & acquire up to 50 million shares in a strategic token initiative.
data.description: In Las Vegas, a recent announcement revealed that a board has approved the authorization of up to $100 million for the purpose of buying back stock that is below Net Asset Value. Additionally, they have been authorized to repurchase up to 50 million shares.

data.title: Esri obtains ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification for data security, complying with global standards, ensuring secure practices.
data.description: Esri, a prominent company in location intelligence based in Redlands, California, has recently achieved ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification. This certification is significant as it allows Esri's ArcGIS users to adhere to data residency and local regulatory standards. It also guarantees that Esri maintains security practices in line with the International Organization for Standardization (ISO) mandates. The ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification serves as a recognized global framework for setting up, executing, and upholding robust information security management systems.

data.title: James Atwood is the new CEO of Opentrons Labworks, overseeing 10,000 robotic systems worldwide.
data.description: In an announcement today, Opentrons Labworks, Inc., a leading laboratory robotics company focused on AI-driven autonomous science, disclosed the appointment of James Atwood as Chief Executive Officer. Atwood, who took over as CEO in July 2025, previously headed Opentrons' Robotics Business Unit starting in April 2023. During his tenure, the company significantly increased its installed base to over 10,000 robotic systems situated worldwide in academic, biotech, and pharmaceutical laboratories.

data.title: RobCo secures $100 million in funding to boost its industrial robotics platform, focusing on U.S. expansion and enterprise deployments.
data.description: A physical AI-driven robotics company, RobCo, has secured $100 million in funding to further its Physical AI roadmap, enhance enterprise deployments, and strengthen its position in the U.S. market. This Series C funding round is jointly led by Lightspeed Venture Partners and Lingotto Innovation, with support from Sequoia Capital, Greenfield Partners, Kindred Capital, Leitmotif, and The Friedkin Group. These venture capital investors contribute significant expertise in developing technology companies.

data.title: Real estate agents are optimistic with an 11-month high as they anticipate a recovery in 2026.
data.description: Real estate agents in Miami are feeling more optimistic about the market, with expectations reaching an 11-month high according to a recent survey. The survey, conducted by Real, shows that agents are anticipating a strong recovery in 2026. This optimism is a positive sign for the real estate industry in Miami and suggests potential growth in the market.

data.title: Forescout's report shows 84% increase in cyberattacks using OT protocols in 2025, analyzing over 900M attacks globally.
data.description: In San Jose, California, Forescout Technologies Inc., a prominent cybersecurity company, has unveiled its latest "2025 Threat Roundup" report focusing on the global threat landscape and key trends for cyber defenders in 2026. The report, compiled by Forescout Research – Vedere Labs, examined over 900 million attacks worldwide throughout 2025. Noteworthy findings reveal that cyberattacks have become more widespread globally and are increasingly utilizing cloud technology. In 2025, threat actors intensified their efforts in exploiting vulnerabilities at a rapid pace.

data.title: Kodiak Gas Services declares $0.49 dividend per share for Q4 2025, payable on Feb 20, 2026 to shareholders of record by Feb 13.
data.description: Kodiak Gas Services, Inc. (NYSE: KGS) based in The Woodlands, Texas, declared a cash dividend of $0.49 per common stock share for Q4 of 2025. This dividend will be disbursed on February 20, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 13, 2026. In alignment with this, Kodiak Gas Services, LLC will also distribute dividends as per their own policy.
